VersionBadge (#101)

This pull request adds a visible version badge to the Flightdeck web app
UI, displaying the current app version and git hash for easier debugging
and transparency. It includes a new `VersionBadge` component, logic for
injecting version and git hash at build time, and comprehensive tests to
ensure correct behavior across environments.

**User Interface Enhancements**
* Added a `VersionBadge` component next to the app title in the header,
showing the app version and, when appropriate, the git hash for dev and
pre-release builds (`App.tsx`, `VersionBadge.tsx`).
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-d69beff79a8cb8294bbc8ed0f7ccb984740a825ec44db4cc0a5a7b30966ab647R29)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-d69beff79a8cb8294bbc8ed0f7ccb984740a825ec44db4cc0a5a7b30966ab647R165-R168)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-2bbc2177345df1afd049b86c48ed195a633e5a38444622d44f43d5a5ec0072edR1-R25)

**Build and Configuration**
* Updated the Vite config to inject `__APP_VERSION__` and `__GIT_HASH__`
into the app at build time, sourcing the version from the root
`package.json` and the git hash from the current commit
(`vite.config.ts`).
* Updated the Vitest config to define test values for `__APP_VERSION__`
and `__GIT_HASH__`, ensuring predictable test output
(`vitest.config.ts`).

**Testing**
* Added comprehensive tests for the `VersionBadge` component to verify
correct display logic for stable, pre-release, and dev builds, as well
as handling of missing or unknown git hashes (`VersionBadge.test.tsx`).

---------

commit.message:
fix: add light mode CSS variable overrides for proper theme toggle

The sidebar and other components weren't responding to theme toggle
because CSS variables were only defined for dark mode. Light mode had
no variable overrides, so components kept dark values.

Add comprehensive light-mode CSS variable overrides in :where(.light)
selector that swap to the *-light variants:
- --color-bg → --color-bg-light
- --color-border → --color-border-light
- --color-text-* → --color-text-*-light variants
- --color-surface → --color-surface-light

This makes ALL components (sidebar, breadcrumbs, etc.) automatically
respond to theme toggle. Previously only body and prose had overrides.

Now when user clicks theme toggle:
- Main area changes (was working)
- Sidebar changes (now fixed)
- Breadcrumbs change (now fixed)
- All other components change (now fixed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add CSRF protection (synchronizer token pattern)

- app/csrf.py: CSRFMiddleware stores a per-session token in the
  Starlette session; validates X-CSRF-Token header for API calls
  and csrf_token form field for the login form POST
- main.py: register CSRFMiddleware between SessionMiddleware and
  AuthMiddleware so session is populated before validation
- base.html: inject token as <meta name="csrf-token"> for JS access
- login.html: embed token as hidden form field
- api.js: read meta tag and attach X-CSRF-Token header on all
  non-safe (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) requests
- AUTH_DISABLED=1 skips CSRF checks in tests (same bypass as auth)

feat: veto_keywords hook, chaos module scenarios, GAEngine schema fixes

## loop.py — pre-LLM veto_keywords guardrail (p6-code-hook + p6-veto)
- Added pre-LLM content veto gate in _run_loop() before LLM call
- CISO/Compliance/Architect agents can declare veto_keywords in permissions_json
- If incoming message contains a keyword → immediate VETO emitted, LLM skipped
- Deterministic, tamper-resistant: no prompt injection possible on guardrail level
- Source: ADR-0015, inspired by OWASP LLM01 (prompt injection guardrails)

## chaos_endurance.py — module resilience scenarios (chaos-module-scenarios)
- Added SCENARIOS_MODULE list: module_disable_browser_cli, module_disable_rtk, module_disable_redis
- Added _set_module_enabled() helper: toggle module ON/OFF in DB settings
- Added _exec_module_scenario(): disable module → verify health → re-enable → measure MTTR
- Rationale: optional modules MUST NOT crash the platform when Settings toggle is OFF
- Delegates from _exec_scenario() else branch to keep main handler clean

## migrations.py — GAEngine + agents schema (p9-project-id-fk + p10-ga-trigger)
- evolution_proposals: add genome_json, fitness, run_id (code uses these, table had old names)
- evolution_runs: add wf_id, generations, fitness_history_json (same divergence)
- Drop NOT NULL on legacy columns (workflow_id, mutated_config) for coexistence
- agents: add project_id column (links ft-* project agents to their project)
- All migration statements are idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS / DROP IF EXISTS)

Verified: GAEngine.evolve_all() saves 9 proposals + 3 runs to DB correctly.
veto_keywords set on 9 critical agents (CISO, DPO, compliance, architects) via script.
project_id backfilled on 28 ft-* agents.

commit.message:
fix: prevent race condition in batch processor lock release

- Added lock_uuid verification to finally block to prevent clearing another process's lock
- If batch runs >5 minutes, another process can acquire the lock (by design)
- Now ensures only the lock owner can release its own lock, preventing concurrent execution
- Matches the shutdown handler's verification logic (lines 128-137)
- Fixes high-severity race condition identified in code review

commit.message:
test(w71): feature gate + capability reporting tests (~40 tests) (#601)

## Summary

Add comprehensive feature gate boundary tests enforcing the **\"no green
by omission\"** invariant — when optional features
(git/content/walk/halstead) are unavailable, the code must not silently
succeed or produce empty results.

### \crates/tokmd-analysis/tests/feature_gates_w71.rs\ (26 tests)
- **Git feature gate**: risk/git/identity/deep preset git requirements,
\git=false\ override
- **Content feature gate**: health/architecture/deep content-dependent
enrichers
- **Walk feature gate**: supply/security walk-dependent enrichers
- **Receipt baseline**: receipt preset works without any optional
features
- **Health without git**: health preset does not require git
- **Risk degradation**: risk preset degrades gracefully with \git=false\
- **Capability reporting**: preset plan resolution,
\preset_plan_for_name\, \
eeds_files\ consistency
- **Receipt metadata**: schema_version, tool name, mode, warnings,
status
- **Warning contract**: \DisabledFeature\ keywords, git/walk/content
mentions
- **Grid completeness**: all 11 presets covered, deep superset of
receipt

### \crates/tokmd/tests/feature_gates_w71.rs\ (14 tests)
- **Help text**: preset values, format option, git flag visibility
- **JSON envelope**: warnings array, status field, schema_version, tool
name
- **Graceful degradation**: invalid preset fails, \--no-git\
suppression, receipt always succeeds
- **Cross-preset validation**: all presets produce valid JSON with
required fields
- **Receipt exclusion**: receipt JSON excludes optional sections (git,
entropy, assets)

commit.message:
test(w71): BDD scenario tests for user workflows (~30 tests) (#599)

## Summary

Adds 38 BDD-style scenario tests in
\crates/tokmd/tests/bdd_scenarios_w71.rs\ following Given/When/Then
naming conventions.

### Test Coverage

| # | Scenario | Description |
|---|----------|-------------|
| 1-3 | Language detection | Rust project, multi-language, empty dir |
| 4-5 | Module & exclude | Exclude patterns, module depth grouping |
| 6 | Export CSV | All files listed in CSV |
| 7 | Diff | Two receipts produce diff output |
| 8 | Exclude flag | CLI --exclude applies during scan |
| 9 | Unicode | Unicode filenames normalized |
| 10-11 | Ignore rules | Hidden files excluded, .gitignore respected |
| 12-15 | JSON validity | Valid JSON + schema_version for
lang/module/export/analyze |
| 16-19 | Determinism | Identical output on repeated runs |
| 20-22 | check-ignore | Explains why files are excluded |
| 23-32 | Misc commands | Badge, init, tools, completions, version, TSV,
no-ignore-vcs |

### Approach

- All tests use hermetic temp directories with \.git\ markers
- Deterministic: no flaky assertions, no time-dependent checks
- BDD naming: \given_X_when_Y_then_Z\
- Uses existing \common::fixture_root()\ for shared fixtures
